rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the GT's actual concern (kernels<0.15 lacking a lower bound so trust_remote_code would break on 0.13.x), but both notice the adjacent description/diff mismatch around trust_remote_code, so recall is equally partial. X is more thorough and equally well-grounded: it adds a concrete missing-test-for-caching finding with a specific proposed assertion, flags the lock-serialization issue, the debuggability regression in log messages, and a minor dead-init nit — all valid, evidence-cited, and actionable with file/line anchors. Y raises only the same trust_remote_code and lock-serialization points, and explicitly (and less convincingly) waves off the missing-cache-test concern as unnecessary, making it less thorough despite being well-written.
